name: balance
description: Return the current credit balance and subscription status for the authenticated AI Patent Search Generator account. Use this before calling paid tools (dossier, oa_analyze, query, search) to verify credits are available.

name: dossier
description: Fetch a comprehensive intelligence dossier for a patent by publication number. Returns bibliographic data, independent claims, backward + forward citations, patent family, CPC classifications, examiner info, and similar documents. Costs 3 credits on a fresh fetch; subsequent calls within 24 hours read from cache and are free. The headline tool - most patent-analysis workflows start here.

patentNumber.description: Publication number in canonical form (e.g. US10867416B2, EP3500001B1). Accepts loose input - the server normalizes.

name: prosecution
description: Retrieve the USPTO file-wrapper documents (office actions, responses, amendments, IDS, notices) for a US patent or application. Free. Returns each documents ID, mail date, category, and short description - IDs can be passed to oa_analyze for AI analysis.

patentNumber.description: Patent publication number (e.g. US10867416B2). One of patentNumber or applicationNumber required.

applicationNumber.description: USPTO application number (e.g. 15912345). One of patentNumber or applicationNumber required.

name: oa_analyze
description: AI-analyze a USPTO Office Action document to extract rejection grounds (102, 103, 112, etc.), cited prior art, and suggested response arguments. Two invocation forms: (1) pass only patentNumber and the server auto-picks the most recent Office Action; (2) pass both applicationNumber and documentId for explicit selection. First 5 analyses per application are free; subsequent analyses cost 1 credit each.

patentNumber.description: Patent publication number - auto-picks the most recent OA for this patent.

applicationNumber.description: Application number for explicit selection (used with documentId).

documentId.description: Office Action document ID from prosecution-history output (used with applicationNumber).

name: examiner
description: Get the assigned examiners name, art unit, total applications handled, allowance rate, and average pendency for a US patent. Free. Useful for prosecution strategy and risk assessment.

name: query
description: Generate a single optimized Boolean search query for Google Patents from a natural-language description of an invention or technology. Returns the Boolean string only - does NOT execute the search. Use search if you want ranked hits instead. Costs 1 credit.

description.description: Natural-language description of the invention or technology area. Minimum 10 characters.

name: search
description: Execute a patent search against Google Patents server-side and return ranked hits. Given a natural-language description and a search strategy, the server: (1) extracts technical concepts, (2) generates Boolean queries per the chosen strategy, (3) runs each query against Google Patents, (4) dedupes results by publication number. Strategies: telescoping (3 queries: broad/moderate/narrow - recommended default), onion-ring (layered queries adding one concept at a time), faceted (multiple two-concept pair queries). Costs 1 credit.

description.description: Natural-language description of the invention or technology area (minimum 10 characters).

limit.description: Maximum number of deduplicated hits to return (default 20, max 100).

name: similar
description: Return Google Patents similar documents ranking for a given patent - a list of patents that Google considers technically related based on its own semantic models. Free. Distinct from search (which generates new queries from a description); this tool finds patents related to an existing one.

limit.description: Maximum number of similar patents to return (default 20).

name: citations
description: Return backward and/or forward citations for a patent. Backward = patents this one cites (its prior art). Forward = patents that cite this one (downstream impact). Each citation includes whether it was examiner-cited (load-bearing on patentability) vs applicant-cited. Free. Lighter payload than dossier when you only need citation lists.

direction.description: Which citation set to return. Defaults to both.

name: family
description: Return the patent family for a given patent - continuations, divisionals, and national counterparts (foreign equivalents). Useful for confirming worldwide IP coverage and identifying related applications still pending. Free.

name: cpc
description: Look up a Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) code and return its section, class, subclass, and group context. Accepts any level of code - section letter (e.g. H), class (H01), subclass (H01M), main group (H01M10/00), or subgroup (H01M10/0525). Free. v1.0 dataset covers all sections + ~80 common subclasses; subgroup-level descriptions land in v1.1.

code.description: CPC code at any level (section, class, subclass, main group, or subgroup).
